title: with_ecc_key closure pattern correctly guarantees key cleanup on error
description: wolftpm/src/device.rs:100: with_ecc_key uses a closure + Drop to guarantee the TPM transient object handles are flushed even if the caller's closure returns Err. This is the correct RAII pattern for TPM transient objects: the TPM has a finite number of transient object slots, so leaking handles under error conditions would eventually cause TPM_RC_OBJECT_MEMORY. The EccKey::Drop impl ensures flush always runs. A contributor might suggest returning EccKey directly (more flexible) — that design should be defended only if the user can be trusted to call flush/drop explicitly, which they cannot in practice.
